goal:
Decision GLOBAL-D-05-02 reference: TUI is the operator's interactive coordinator (chat + dispatch + viewer + commands), not a viewer. It must NEVER refuse to start. Multi-instance allowed; if a router is already running for this workspace, attach + share state instead of competing.

REPRO (2026-04-22): operator ran 'gad tui'. Got: '[router] lockfile exists at C:/Users/benja/Documents/custom_portfolio/.gad/router-logs/.lock — pid=26520 is still running.' Verified PID 26520 NOT alive (tasklist empty). Lockfile timestamp 5h+ stale. Operator forced to manually 'rm .gad/router-logs/.lock' to launch. ERRORS entry: gad-tui-stale-router-lockfile-blocks-launch-2026-04-22.

ROOT CAUSE: TUI is currently bundled with router (start TUI → start router → singleton lock at .gad/router-logs/.lock). Two problems: (a) lockfile orphans when prior TUI crashes without cleanup, (b) operator can't peek at active workers while another TUI session is open (prevents the 'I just want to look while claude works' flow).

FIX SCOPE: split TUI from router. Three pieces:
(1) Router becomes a standalone singleton process per workspace (or per-project — see 63-24). Lockfile still valid for router, but with eager liveness probe before refusing: parse pid from lockfile, probe via process.kill(pid, 0) on POSIX or 'tasklist /FI "PID eq <pid>"' on Windows. If pid dead OR lockfile age > N seconds AND pid dead, log '[router] stale lockfile (pid=X dead, age=Ns) — clearing' and unlink + proceed.
(2) TUI becomes standalone — no lockfile of its own. On launch: probe for running router (read .gad/router-logs/.lock, check liveness). If running: ATTACH (tail router-logs, talk to router via IPC — named pipe / unix socket / shared file). If not running: spawn one as detached background process AND open TUI as separate viewer.
(3) Files to inspect: packages/gad-tui/src/router.ts (lockfile logic at lines 18, 19, 54, 55, 256, 257, 356), packages/gad-tui/src/server/serve.ts, packages/gad-tui/src/server/TuiServer.ts.

EDGE CASES:
- Lockfile exists, pid alive, lockfile age < 60s: another TUI just opened; attach.
- Lockfile exists, pid alive, lockfile age > some threshold: still attach (long-running TUI is normal — operator left it open).
- Lockfile exists, pid dead, lockfile age any: clear + start fresh router + open TUI.
- Lockfile missing: start fresh router + open TUI.
- Multiple TUIs concurrently: all attach to the same router; each sees same state.
- IPC failure (router alive but doesn't respond): timeout after Ns, log warning, fall back to read-only mode (TUI still opens, just can't dispatch).

ACCEPTANCE: 'gad tui' command never exits with a lockfile-related error. Two concurrent 'gad tui' processes both render full state. Killing one doesn't affect the other. After all TUIs close, router stays running until explicit 'gad router stop' or workspace close.

PID-FILE SWEEP (separate audit): all vendor pid-file consumers (gad team dispatcher .planning/team/dispatcher.pid, possibly others) need the same eager-probe pattern. Grep for 'pidfile\|\.pid\|lockfile\|\.lock' in vendor/get-anything-done/lib + bin to find them.

REFERENCES:
- ERRORS: gad-tui-stale-router-lockfile-blocks-launch-2026-04-22 (open, blocks this task)
- DECISION: GLOBAL-D-05-02 — every project is a GAD project, TUI is the unified coordinator
- MEMORY: project_phase_64_tui_orchestrator (TUI is operator's primary interactive surface)
